title: Best AI Audio Tools for Podcast Production in 2026
description: Compare 8 best AI podcast production tools in 2026. Recording, noise removal, editing, mastering, and transcription — find your ideal workflow.
content:
Podcasting has matured into a $30 billion industry, but the production workflow remains the single biggest friction point for most creators. Between recording remote guests, removing background noise, editing out filler words, generating transcripts, mixing audio levels, and publishing to multiple platforms, a single 30-minute episode can consume 4–6 hours of post-production labor. In 2026, AI audio tools have finally crossed the threshold where they meaningfully automate the entire podcast production pipeline — not just one or two steps.

The most significant shift this year is the emergence of end-to-end AI podcast platforms that handle recording, editing, mastering, transcription, and distribution in a single workflow. These tools reduce the average post-production time from 4 hours to under 30 minutes per episode, while simultaneously improving audio quality through AI-powered noise cancellation, leveling, and mastering algorithms that rival professional studio hardware.

This guide evaluates eight leading AI audio tools for podcast production, comparing their capabilities across the full production chain: recording, noise removal, editing, mastering, transcription, and show notes generation.

For a broader view of audio AI tools, browse our [[link:/category/audio|Audio AI tools category]]. If you are comparing specific tools head-to-head, check out our [[link:/compare/audio|audio tools comparison page]].

---

## The Modern Podcast Production Pipeline

Before evaluating individual tools, it is important to understand the six stages of podcast production that AI can now automate or assist with:

1. **Recording**: Capturing high-quality audio from local and remote participants, with automatic level management and backup recording.
2. **Noise Removal**: Eliminating background hum, room echo, keyboard clicks, traffic noise, and other environmental artifacts without degrading voice quality.
3. **Editing**: Cutting silent gaps, removing filler words ("um," "uh," "you know"), correcting mistakes, and tightening pacing — traditionally the most time-consuming step.
4. **Mastering**: Applying EQ, compression, loudness normalization (to -16 LUFS for podcast standards), and stereo enhancement to produce a polished, broadcast-ready sound.
5. **Transcription**: Converting speech to text with high accuracy, speaker diarization (identifying who said what), and timestamp generation for show notes.
6. **Distribution and Promotion**: Publishing to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube, and generating audiograms, social media clips, and show notes.

A truly effective AI podcast tool should address at least four of these six stages. The tools below vary in their scope — some are specialized point solutions, while others are comprehensive platforms.

---

## Top 8 AI Audio Tools for Podcast Production in 2026

### 1. Descript — The Text-Based Editing Revolution

Descript remains the category-defining tool for AI-powered podcast editing. Its core innovation — editing audio by editing the transcript — has been refined through six major releases and now supports 23 languages with over 95% transcription accuracy.

**Key Features:**
- Text-based editing: delete a sentence in the transcript, and Descript removes it from the audio — complete with automatic crossfade and gap removal
- Overdub: AI voice cloning that lets you type corrections in your own voice to fix mistakes without re-recording
- Studio Sound: one-click AI audio enhancement that removes room echo, background noise, and boosts clarity as if recorded in a professional studio
- Filler word removal: automatically detects and removes "um," "uh," "you know," and "like" with one click
- Screen recording and video editing integrated into the same workflow
- AI Actions: natural language commands like "remove all silences longer than 2 seconds" or "add a fade-out to the last 5 seconds"

**Pricing:** Free tier (1 hour transcription/month). Creator at $24/month (10 hours), Business at $40/month (30 hours). Enterprise plans available.

**Best for:** Podcasters who want the fastest possible editing workflow and are comfortable with a text-based editing paradigm.

[Try Descript Free →](https://www.descript.com)

### 2. Riverside FM — Studio-Quality Remote Recording

Riverside has become the gold standard for remote podcast recording. Unlike Zoom or Google Meet, which compress audio for bandwidth efficiency, Riverside records each participant's audio and video locally on their device and uploads it progressively — resulting in lossless, studio-quality tracks regardless of internet connection quality.

**Key Features:**
- Local recording: 48kHz WAV audio and 4K video per participant, unaffected by internet fluctuations
- Separate tracks: each guest's audio and video is recorded independently, enabling per-track editing in post-production
- AI transcription: automatic transcription in 100+ languages with speaker diarization (who said what)
- Magic Clips: AI identifies the most engaging moments from your recording and generates short-form social media clips
- AI show notes: automatically generates episode summaries, timestamps, and key takeaways
- Progressive upload: recordings begin uploading during the session, so they are available immediately after ending

**Pricing:** Free tier (2 hours recording/month, separate audio tracks). Standard at $19/month (5 hours), Pro at $29/month (15 hours). Business plans at custom pricing.

**Best for:** Podcasters who prioritize recording quality and regularly host remote guests.

[Try Riverside FM →](https://www.riverside.fm)

### 3. Adobe Podcast — Studio-Grade AI Enhancement

Adobe Podcast, formerly known as Project Sharly, has rapidly evolved into one of the most impressive AI audio enhancement tools on the market. Its speech enhancement model is trained on a massive dataset of studio-quality recordings, enabling it to transform recordings made in noisy environments into audio that sounds like it was captured in a professional studio.

**Key Features:**
- Enhance Speech: one-click AI processing that removes background noise, echo, and reverb while preserving natural voice timbre — the best-in-class single-pass enhancer
- Mic Check: AI analyzes your microphone setup and room acoustics, providing specific recommendations for improvement before recording
- Remote recording with local-quality tracks via the browser
- AI-powered transcription with speaker labeling
- Direct integration with Adobe Audition and Premiere Pro for advanced editing workflows

**Pricing:** Free tier available (limited Enhance Speech uses). Adobe Express Premium at $9.99/month. Full Creative Cloud at $54.99/month.

**Best for:** Podcasters who need the best possible AI noise removal and audio enhancement, especially those already in the Adobe ecosystem.

[Try Adobe Podcast →](https://podcast.adobe.com)

### 4. Podcastle AI — All-in-One Content Studio

Podcastle has evolved from a podcast recording tool into a comprehensive AI content studio. Its strength lies in bundling recording, editing, mastering, and AI voice generation into a single platform — making it particularly appealing for solo podcasters who want to minimize tool switching.

**Key Features:**
- Multi-track recording with local-quality audio for remote guests
- Magic Dust: one-click AI mastering that applies EQ, compression, and loudness normalization to broadcast standards
- AI noise removal with per-track intensity control
- Revoice: AI voice cloning for generating pickups, corrections, or entirely new segments without re-recording
- AI-powered silence removal and filler word detection
- Built-in text-to-speech with 30+ realistic AI voices for intros, outros, and ad reads

**Pricing:** Free tier (1 hour recording/month, basic editing). Storyteller at $11.99/month (5 hours, AI features). Pro at $23.99/month (20 hours, full AI access).

**Best for:** Solo podcasters looking for an all-in-one platform that covers recording through publishing.

[Try Podcastle AI →](https://podcastle.ai)

### 5. Auphonic — Automated Audio Mastering Specialist

Auphonic takes a different approach: it does not handle recording or editing, but it is unmatched at automated audio post-production. For podcasters who already have their recording and editing workflow established, Auphonic serves as the final mastering stage, ensuring every episode meets professional loudness and quality standards.

**Key Features:**
- Intelligent loudness normalization to -16 LUFS (Apple Podcasts standard), -14 LUFS (Spotify), or custom targets
- Automatic leveling of multiple speakers — ensures a soft-spoken guest and a loud host are balanced to the same perceived volume
- Noise and hum reduction with machine learning-based detection of environmental noise patterns
- Adaptive noise gate, high-pass filter, and dynamic compression applied intelligently per track
- Chapter mark generation from audio analysis
- Multi-format export: MP3, AAC, Opus, FLAC, WAV, with metadata and chapter marks embedded

**Pricing:** Free tier (2 hours/month). Monthly plans start at $11/month for 9 hours, $24/month for 20 hours. Pay-as-you-go credits available.

**Best for:** Podcasters who have a recording workflow they like and need a dedicated, best-in-class mastering tool.

[Try Auphonic →](https://auphonic.com)

### 6. ElevenLabs — AI Voice Generation and Audio Enhancement

ElevenLabs has expanded beyond its roots in text-to-speech to become a versatile audio AI platform. For podcasters, its value proposition lies in three areas: generating professional voiceovers for intros, outros, and ad reads; voice cloning for corrections; and AI-powered audio enhancement.

**Key Features:**
- Industry-leading AI voice generation with 1,000+ voices across 29 languages — realistic enough for podcast intros, sponsor reads, and narrative segments
- Professional Voice Cloning: create a digital replica of your voice for corrections, pickups, or content repurposing without re-recording
- AI dubbing: translate podcast content into other languages with voice preservation
- Speech-to-Speech: transform low-quality recordings into studio-quality output by processing through the voice generation pipeline
- Projects: a long-form audio editor for combining generated and recorded audio into a final episode

**Pricing:** Free tier (10,000 characters/month). Starter at $5/month (30,000 characters), Creator at $22/month (100,000 characters), Pro at $99/month (500,000 characters).

**Best for:** Podcasters who want to add professional AI voiceovers, create multilingual versions, or use voice cloning for corrections.

[Try ElevenLabs →](https://elevenlabs.io)

### 7. Murf AI — Studio Voiceovers and Podcast Intros

Murf AI specializes in AI-generated voiceovers that sound indistinguishable from human narration. While it is not a full podcast editing platform, it fills a specific need for podcasters: creating polished intros, outros, transitions, and sponsored segments without hiring voice talent.

**Key Features:**
- 120+ AI voices across 20 languages, each with adjustable pitch, speed, emphasis, and pauses
- Voice cloning with 2-minute reference audio for custom voice generation
- Built-in background music library with royalty-free tracks for podcast intros and segues
- Timeline-based editor for synchronizing AI voiceover with background music and sound effects
- Direct video export for creating podcast audiograms and promotional clips

**Pricing:** Free tier (10 minutes voice generation). Basic at $19/month (24 hours/year), Pro at $39/month (96 hours/year), Enterprise at custom pricing.

**Best for:** Podcasters who need professional-sounding voiceovers for intros, outros, and ad reads without hiring voice talent.

[Try Murf AI →](https://murf.ai)

### 8. Cleanvoice AI — Automated Filler Word and Silence Removal

Cleanvoice AI is a focused tool that does one thing exceptionally well: cleaning up podcast audio by removing filler words, stutters, mouth sounds, and long silences. It is designed to be a drop-in step in any existing podcast workflow — upload your raw audio, let Cleanvoice process it, and download a clean version ready for finishing.

**Key Features:**
- Filler word removal: detects and removes "um," "ah," "er," "you know," "like," and "I mean" in 20+ languages
- Mouth sound removal: eliminates clicks, pops, and saliva sounds that are tedious to remove manually
- Silence removal: intelligently trims long pauses without making the conversation feel rushed or unnatural
- Stutter detection: identifies and smooths out stutters and verbal false starts
- Multi-language support: works across English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, and more

**Pricing:** Pay-as-you-go: $0.10 per minute of processed audio. Subscription: $20/month for 300 minutes, $40/month for 750 minutes.

**Best for:** Podcasters who already have a DAW editing workflow and want a specialized tool to handle the most tedious cleanup tasks.

[Try Cleanvoice AI →](https://cleanvoice.ai)

---

## Comparison Table

| Tool | Recording | Noise Removal | AI Editing | Mastering | Transcription | Starting Price | Best For |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Descript | Screen + mic | Studio Sound | Text-based editing, filler removal | Basic leveling | 95%+ accuracy, 23 languages | $24/month | Fastest editing workflow |
| Riverside FM | Local 48kHz WAV/4K | Basic noise suppression | Magic Clips, AI show notes | Not included | 100+ languages, diarization | $19/month | Remote recording quality |
| Adobe Podcast | Browser-based | Enhance Speech (best-in-class) | Not included | Not included | Speaker labeling | Free / $9.99/month | Noise removal excellence |
| Podcastle AI | Multi-track local | Per-track AI noise removal | Silence removal, filler removal | Magic Dust mastering | AI transcription | $11.99/month | All-in-one solo creator |
| Auphonic | Not included | ML-based noise/hum reduction | Not included | LUFS normalization, leveling | Not included | $11/month | Dedicated mastering |
| ElevenLabs | Not included | Speech-to-Speech enhancement | Voice cloning for corrections | Not included | Not included | $5/month | AI voice generation |
| Murf AI | Not included | Not included | Timeline voiceover editor | Not included | Not included | $19/month | Professional voiceovers |
| Cleanvoice AI | Not included | Not included | Filler word, silence, mouth sound removal | Not included | Not included | $0.10/min | Audio cleanup specialist |

---

## Recommended Podcast Production Workflows

### The Solo Podcaster (Budget-Conscious)

**Recommended Stack:** Riverside FM (recording) → Cleanvoice AI (cleanup) → Auphonic (mastering)

This combination gives you studio-quality remote recording, automated filler word and silence removal, and broadcast-standard mastering for under $40/month total. The trade-off is that you will need to do some manual editing between Cleanvoice and Auphonic, but the AI tools handle the most time-consuming steps.

**Estimated production time per 30-minute episode:** 20–25 minutes.

### The Solo Podcaster (All-in-One)

**Recommended Stack:** Podcastle AI or Descript (end-to-end)

Both tools cover recording, editing, mastering, and transcription in a single platform. Podcastle is better for creators who prefer a traditional timeline editor. Descript is better for those who embrace text-based editing. Either choice keeps your monthly cost under $25 and eliminates tool switching.

**Estimated production time per 30-minute episode:** 15–20 minutes.

### The Professional Podcast Team

**Recommended Stack:** Riverside FM (recording) → Descript (editing + transcription) → Adobe Podcast (enhancement) → Auphonic (final mastering)

This workflow uses the best tool for each stage and produces broadcast-quality output. The cost is higher — approximately $80–$120/month depending on plan tiers — but the quality and efficiency gains justify the investment for shows with sponsorships or significant audiences.

**Estimated production time per 30-minute episode:** 25–30 minutes (with a producer handling the toolchain).

### The Audiogram-Focused Creator

**Recommended Stack:** Riverside FM (recording) → Descript (editing) → Murf AI (AI voiceovers) → Canva/CapCut (audiogram creation)

This workflow prioritizes social media promotion. Riverside's Magic Clips and Descript's clip export make it easy to generate short-form content, while Murf AI adds professional voiceover intros to clip compilations.

**Estimated production time per episode + clips:** 30–40 minutes.

---

## Key Considerations When Choosing AI Podcast Tools

### Transcription Accuracy

If you publish show notes or blog posts based on your episodes, transcription accuracy is critical. Descript (95%+ accuracy) and Riverside FM (100+ languages) lead the pack. Cleanvoice AI and Auphonic do not offer transcription, so factor that into your workflow.

### Audio Quality vs. Convenience

Adobe Podcast's Enhance Speech produces the best single-pass noise removal, but it requires you to upload audio to Adobe's cloud. Riverside FM records lossless audio locally but requires guests to use a browser. Weigh the trade-off between quality and convenience for your specific use case.

### Voice Cloning Ethics

Several tools on this list — Descript, Podcastle, and ElevenLabs — offer voice cloning. This is a powerful feature for correcting mistakes, but it raises ethical considerations about consent and disclosure. Always obtain explicit permission from guests before using voice cloning on their audio, and consider adding a disclosure to episodes where AI-generated voice segments are used.

### Platform Lock-In

All-in-one platforms like Podcastle and Descript are convenient, but they create a degree of lock-in. If you ever want to switch tools, migrating your project files and edit history can be challenging. Using a modular workflow (recording tool + editing tool + mastering tool) gives you more flexibility to swap individual components as tools improve.

---

## The 2026 Podcast Production Landscape

The podcast production tool market has consolidated around a few key players in 2026, but the gap between tools continues to narrow. The competitive differentiators are shifting from "who has AI features" to "whose AI features are most accurate and well-integrated." Descript leads on editing speed, Riverside FM on recording quality, and Adobe Podcast on audio enhancement — but the second-place tools in each category are closing the gap.

For podcasters, this is an unequivocally positive development. The cost of producing a professional-quality podcast has dropped from $5,000+ in equipment and software (2019) to under $50/month in subscription fees (2026). The AI tools handle the technical heavy lifting, freeing creators to focus on what matters: telling great stories and building an audience.

---

## Final Recommendation

If you are starting a podcast in 2026 from scratch, begin with Descript for its unmatched editing speed and transcription accuracy. As your show grows, add Riverside FM for remote guest recording quality and Auphonic for broadcast-standard mastering. For creators who produce narrative or documentary-style podcasts, supplement with ElevenLabs or Murf AI for professional voiceover segments.

The tools are ready. The audience is waiting. Start recording.

**Try any of the tools above with free trials and find the workflow that fits your podcast.**
